philosophy: Top-down regime mapping. Expresses directional, largely unhedged convictions via global liquidity metrics, sovereign yield curves, and central bank reaction functions. Does not pick stocks — trades regimes.
leverage_estimate: 80% Gross (High Notional via Derivatives and Futures)

factor_exposure.duration: Extreme — rates drive all macro equity pricing
factor_exposure.currency: High — FX is a primary expression vehicle
factor_exposure.commodity: Moderate — used as regime confirmation signal

sector_concentration: None — trades broad indices, curves, and FX pairs only

risk_constraints.forced_liquidation_trigger: Unexpected Central Bank pivot OR thesis invalidation event

preferred_instruments: Sovereign Bonds and Bond Futures (TLT, ZB)

historical_regime_behavior.q4_2018_rate_hike: Aggressively shorted front-end rates via Eurodollar futures. Avoided equity fundamentals entirely. Amplified duration shock.
historical_regime_behavior.march_2020_covid: Initiated massive long duration structural trades (long TLT) as fiscal response predicted rate suppression. Held through equity volatility.
historical_regime_behavior.q3_2022_inflation: Largest short sovereign bond position in decade. Expressed via TLT puts and ZB futures shorts as inflation persistence was confirmed.

macro_reaction_function: Unhedged conviction amplification. During a duration shock (yields spiking), aggressively shorts bond proxies and long-duration tech ETFs — compounds the initial catalyst directionally rather than hedging.
confidence_notes: Moderate Confidence. Discretionary nature introduces human bias and timing risk. However, structural regime bets tend to be vindicated over the 3-12 month horizon.
